rationale: The indexed intersection is defined over an indexed family {A_i} (def:indexed-family) and is restricted to a nonempty index set I (def:empty-set, the I != empty condition that keeps the intersection well-posed). Both were missing from the empty set.
notes: Under-specified []. Defined directly by set-builder ({x | for all i, x in A_i}), so it does NOT depend on the binary def:intersection -- it is the indexed generalization. The I != empty restriction is load-bearing (intersection over an empty index would be the universe). licensing_quote grounds def:empty-set; def:indexed-family is the family operand.
